characters.name: Narrator
characters.voiceDesc: A seasoned storyteller with a rich, resonant voice that combines gravitas and warmth. Commanding yet soothing, this voice delivers each word with clarity and intention, making it ideal for immersive storytelling. Its steady, engaging pace captures attention and leaves a lasting impression.

lines.text: This seems like a quiet little town.  On the porch ahead of you are two people.  The standing one is large, rather ugly, and playing with a yo-yo.  The seated person smoking a pipe looks like he might be the Sheriff.
The Sheriff's office doubles as a jail for the town and a hangout for the Sheriff and the Goon.
The Sign of the Scissors above the door, along with the striped pole, indicates that the building contains a Barber Shop.
The windows glow from the light inside. The sign reads "Hero's Tale Inn".
There are signs over each of the buildings informing you of their purpose.
The town gate is standing wide open, as it does every day.
Spielburg remains colorful, even at night.
The shades are drawn, and it appears that the Inn is closed.
The town gate is made of sturdy logs.
The door appears to be locked, and breaking into the Sheriff's office might evoke the wrath of Otto Von Goon.
You are carrying so much that you can hardly move.  You'd better drop something soon.
You can't get that.
There aren't any here.
You don't have time -- there's the slight matter of the monster that wants to eat you.
You can't see it.
Turn around.
That's the Sheriff's job.
Searching a law enforcer is a bad idea.
The door is locked.
It stands open.

The software you are now using was produced
through the efforts of many people:
designers, artists, programmers, musicians,
and lots of other hard-working folks.

If you make copies of this software for any
reason other than to make a perso
Otto and the Sheriff have retired for the evening.
There's no hint of the Ogre.
You see no sign of the Sheriff.
A typical barber's pole.
It is closed, locked, and barred.
There's a sign on the door of the barber shop, informing whoever reads it that the barber is out to lunch.  The door is locked.
The barber must keep odd lunch hours.
There appears to be a note on the door of the barber shop.
You'd better not. The Goon would probably try to strangle you with his yo-yo if he thought you were going into the Sheriff's office without permission.
There's a sign on the door of the barber's shop, informing whoever reads it that the barber is out to lunch. The door is locked.
The gate is already closed.
The sturdy town gate is completely locked and barred, as it is every evening.
Only the Sheriff can unlock the gate.
The Barber Shop appears to be closed. There's a note hanging from the doorknob.
The man with the pipe greets you.
There's more than a hint of Ogre about this strange and bulky character. He seems cheerful, though.
Schultz appears to be an affable old coot, content to smoke his pipe. There's a hardness behind his eyes, however, that gives you reason to believe that he's no pushover.
It goes up and down...up and down...up and down...up and down.....
The Sheriff's pipe is a truly impressive hand-carved ivory pipe.
The Sheriff informs you that he's much too busy for idle chit-chat.
Otto only expresses himself physically.
However, this was not a good place to sleep.  You're stiff and sore all over and your wallet feels very light.
You pay the Healer for the potion and put it away.
You find that you have less money than you thought.  You can't afford to buy the potion.
You can buy potions, get information, or sell ingredients here.
You see a clean, but very cluttered little hut filled with bottles and potions.  The Healer is busy puttering around with her potions.
There is a small flying creature preening itself .  A ladder leads to the loft above.  A pot over the fire simmers with herbs.  It is pleasantly warm in here.
You see a middle-aged woman with rosy cheeks and smile lines around her eyes.
You see a winged lizard much like the one you saw outside.
You see bottles and flasks of a wide variety of different potions.
Whatever is boiling there is either soup or some sort of potion.
Soup is good for you.  Mmmm Mmmm good.
It looks like a ladder.
It's not your soup.
Don't be nosy.
You can look but don't touch.
A rather motherly-looking woman with a look of vagueness that belies her shrewdness.  She wears an apron over her dress and a scarf wrapped around her head.
The Healer makes some sort of arcane gesture over the flask of water, then looks surprised.
The Healer makes some sort of arcane gesture over each flask of water, then smiles.

characters.name: Fairy
characters.voiceDesc: A sweet, cheerful fairy voice, light and sparkly—perfect for charming elves, magical creatures, or whimsical game characters.

lines.text: Dance!
He's no fun!  He fell right over!
Ooh, looky.  A human!
What's it doing here?
Yuck!
Let's have some fun with it.
Humans!  Why is it always humans?
Go away.  You can't play!
I think it wants to play with us.
Human, do you know how to dance?
How do you dance?
Dance?
Dance??
Will you dance for us?
Can you dance?
Then dance for us!
Humans can't dance.
Let's see you dance.
I bet you can't dance!
Dance for us!
All he wants to do is dance.
Let's see you!
Dance!!
We can show it how, can't we?
We know how to make you dance.
You can dance.
We love to teach you how to dance.
How to dance.
Dance?  Dance?
Dance?  Dance!
Dance!  Dance!
Dance!  Dance!  Dance!
DanceDanceDanceDance!!
Hee, Hee!
That's a dance?
What can you expect from a human?
Well, I think it's cute.
You think anything is cute!
Ha, ha, ha!
Ooooh!
It does know how to dance.
Wowie!
This is neat!
Gee, I didn't think it could do this.
Well, I just love it!
You love an Antwerp!
Do not!!
Do too!
So what do you want?
Why are you here?
Do you suppose it's hungry?
What do humans eat?
Fairies!
Fairies?
Everyone knows humans eat animals.
Yuckie!
What are you bothering us for?
Why don't humans ever answer?
What is this all about?
What do you want from us?

characters.name: Erasmus the Wizard
characters.voiceDesc: A timeless arcane voice echoing across centuries of magic and mystery.This 800+ year-old wizard exudes legendary presence with a voice steeped in ancient knowledge, gentle authority, and enchanted charisma. From rumbling incantations to soft-spoken truths, his delivery moves between majestic and intimate, perfect for fantasy narration, RPG storytelling, and mystical worlds. Ideal for audiobooks, cinematic trailers, and epic quests, this voice brings gravitas, warmth, and that unmistakable spark of true wizardry to any project.

lines.text:
Do you know what you get when a Tyranno-
saurus running eastward meets a Tyranno-
saurus running westward?
That's a smashing good joke, I think.

Tyranno-
saurus wrecks!
Is it true that a monster can't hurt you if you carry a torch?
Actually, it depends on how fast you carry it!
Do you know what time it is when Otto walks through your door?
So you've met Otto already.
Time to get a new door!
Do you know the difference between a Cheetaur and a comma?
Spoilsport!
A Cheetaur has claws at the end of its paws, and a comma is a pause at the end of a clause!

Do you know what's big, purple, and eats ungefaehr-
liche dinge?
Well...let me figure out something tougher.

Big, purple ungefaehr-
liche dinge eaters!

Large purple ungefaehr-
liche dinge eaters are all too common in the forest these days.
Do you know which has more legs, one cow or no cow?
Too easy, huh?  Well, I'll just have to think of a better one.
You're right!  NO cow has more than four legs.  These are too easy.
Do you know the difference between a wounded Triceratops and a magic user casting the "Fly" spell?
Darn.  I thought I made that one up myself!
One is a raging Saurus and the other is a soaring Magus!
Sorry, Fenrus, but "He who tells the jokes gets to make up the punch lines".
Did you hear the joke about the witches broom?
Oh.  That's a pity!
That's odd.  It's sweeping the valley!
"I'M BAAD!"
Never met her.  I only built this vacation home here a century ago.
Erana has a nice little magical meadow in her name northeast of here.  Pretty respectable Spellcaster, I imagine.
The Protection spell she cast over the town is fairly good, but I believe she missed a few places.
I thought that "Erasmus' Rootin' Tootin' Root Beer" was a WONDERFUL name for a product!  It's a shame the company went broke after that.
Erana's spell prevents the use of excessive violence or hostile magic within Spielburg's walls, with the exception of a couple of places.
It looks to me from up here as if she missed the back part of the alley, and the spell applies only to areas in town above the ground.
Nobody asked you.
Zara has a real flair for the theatric.  Her method of greeting customers at her shop is showy but effective.
It's a pity she has no sense of humor.

Baba Yaga is good at curses and shape-
changing spells.  She has a nasty temper, so it's best to stay on her good side.
You have to watch her.  She cheats at cards.
She started it!  I just wanted to give her a taste of her own medicine.
The curse Baba Yaga placed upon the Baron von Spielburg was a fine example of creative cursing.
"Upon von Spielburg and all his clan, this Curse I now demand:   What I will shall come full measure, so shall ye lose all that ye treasure."
Of course, the problem is that for every curse there is an equal and opposite countercurse.
A countercurse is the cure for a curse.  It usually works against the one who cast the curse in the first place.
The countercurse for the curse on the Baron goes:
"Come a hero from the East..."
"Free the man from in the beast..."
"Bring the child from out the band..."
"Drive the Curser from the land."
So, to break the curse, a hero must get rid of Baba Yaga, in addition to all the other things.  It does not reflect well on Baba Yaga.
Henry is a hermit who lives by the Flying Falls to the south. Great sense of humor.
I think he borrowed something the last time I saw him, but I can't remember exactly what it was.
Oh, well, Henry is a great one to talk to.
You need to complete your quest here before you can become initiated as a Wizard.
There are many types of magic mirrors.  One of the more common types is the one that has a strange face popping up every so often.
That's not a magic mirror.
At any rate, I used to have a nice magic hand mirror, but I misplaced it somewhere.  It could be used to reflect a spell back upon the caster.
So, if you happen upon a small golden magic mirror with a dragon on the handle, you'll know it's mine.
Oh.  That's right!
Well, if you find a magic mirror with a face on it, you'll know it reflects spells.
Basically, anyone who uses magic is a magic user;   anyone who casts spells is a spellcaster.
However, in order to be a wizard, you need to have undergone initiation into the "Wizard's Institute of Technocery" in the South.

characters.name: Gate Guard
characters.voiceDesc: Rugged and intimate, this baritone voice carries the weight of experience and hardship. Perfect for entertainment and TV, adding depth and authenticity to any role.

lines.text: "Please step where I can see you."
"If ya want a good time, try the 'Dragon's Breath' at the bar."
"Come in.  Come in.  You have been expected.  Baron von Spielburg awaits you in the castle."
"Are you speaking to me?"
"Who's there? Come out and show yourself!"
"Well?  Enter the castle grounds!"
"As you can see, the gate is open."
"I will not open for a faceless voice. Come out where I can see you."
"Very well."
"If you wish."
"As you can see, the gate is closed."
"Then go, before I close the portcullis."
"Sorry.  I wasn't listening.  Could you repeat youself?"
"OK."
"My name is Karl."
"That's me. I guard the portcullis day after day."
"The portcullis is the metal grate that bars the entrance to the castle yard.
"This is the Castle of the Baron Stefan von Spielburg, Lord of this Valley."
"My Lord is busy organizing his troops."
"My Lord sees no one since the loss of his son and daughter."
"Ten years ago the Baron's daughter, Elsa, was stolen away by magic.  Five years back, the Baronet Barnard von Spielburg went on a hunt from which he did not return."
"This was all clearly part of Baba Yaga's curse."
"The Baronet is busy hibernating."
"The Baronet Barnard von Spielburg was a proud young man with the dark hair and eyes of his father, the Baron."

"He rode off one morning and his horse returned without him.  The horse had been raked by the claws of some large animal.  There is still a reward offered for the safe return of the Baronet, but I fear he is dead."
"Elsa von Spielburg was a lovely young child with braided brown hair and bright blue eyes, like her mother, the Baroness, used to have."
"A large winged creature flew over the castle walls, grabbed Elsa, and vanished before the guards could fire upon it.  We searched everywhere for her, but to no avail."
"Even the jester, Yorick, joined the search for her.  The Baron sent a troup of guards to storm Baba Yaga's hut, but I think their skulls sit atop her fence now."
"Elsa von Spielburg would be about eighteen now."

"The stablekeeper could use a strong young person to help clean out the stable.  It does not pay much, but it is good, honest work.  If you would like to take the job, just ask me to open the portcullis."
"Cleaning up after the horses is a good way to build up your muscles."
"The Baron ordered Baba Yaga to leave and she first cursed the graveyard.  When he then sent the Baronial Guards after her, only the captain returned to the castle."

"He spoke the words,
   'Hear me, oh mighty Baron.
You have angered me, and thus you are cursed.
All that you value you shall lose;
all that you treasure shall be taken from you.'
The captain then dissolved into a pool of sticky blackness."
"He was a very jolly fool who loved to laugh.  He was devoted to Elsa and swore he would not return until Elsa was safe."
"The powerful Ogress Baba Yaga is the cause of all the Baron's misfortune.  She is a vile creature who knows much magic."
"There is still a reward of 50 gold offered for the safe return of the Baronet and Elsa von Spielburg."
"For the capture or death of the brigand Leader there is the reward of 60 gold and the title of 'Hero of the Realm of Spielburg'."
"The brigands are protected by a magic user.  Not much is known about him, but he seems to use more magic potions and powders than spells."

"The leader of the brigands must be a good strategist.  He has organized and led many raids which result in much treasure and little loss on the part of the brigands.  Without their leader, the brigands would soon flee."
"There are now too few guards to take out the brigands, who still rob our people and drive away the traders.  They hide somewhere near the edge of this valley."
"This was once a very wealthy valley.  Many merchants have been robbed by the brigands.  There is a merchant in town who could tell you about his recent loss."
"Baba Yaga cursed the graveyard such that the dead can not rest at night.  One cannot go near the graveyard at dark without risking one's own death unless one uses protection."
"There is an unguent you can purchase from the Healer which protects against the dead that walk at night."
"'Hero of the Realm of Spielburg' is a title of respect and honor.  A true hero can release this land from its curse by using intelligence and courage."
"There are many foul creatures stalking the forest.  The Baron has too few guards to protect this valley from them."
"Spielburg Valley was once a prosperous and peaceful place before the Baron lost his family.  Now the Baron will not leave his castle and has no idea of the ruin this land has come to."
"Baba Yaga has her hut to the northwest.  It is a very dangerous place and many Baronial Guards were lost there."
"The Baroness died soon after the birth of her daughter.  The Baron still mourns for her."
"The Baron von Spielburg lost most of his guards trying to defeat Baba Yaga."
"The gatehouse is a shelter and resting place for the guards."
"Everybody talks about the weather, but nobody does anything about it."
"The Weapons Master often practices in the castle courtyard.  He gives lessons in the art of swordplay.  The lessons are expensive, but well worth the price."
"I wouldn't know about that, I don't get around much these days."
"No one enters the castle without the Baron's permission."
"Says you."
"This is the castle of the Baron Stefan von Spielburg."
"The Baron's daughter has been gone for years."
"I'm Frederick."
"The barracks are off to my right, but you have no business going there."
"I can't help you on that."

characters.name: Amelia the Healer
characters.voiceDesc: Middle-aged American female voice, calm and soothing. Perfect for narrations requiring a calming presence.

lines.text: "I cannot make you another Dispel Potion unless you can convince the Dryad to give you another magic acorn.  I do not think that is likely.
Please leave me to make the potion and when you return I shall have it for you.
"If you know the ingredients for a Dispel Potion and bring them here, I can make you such a potion."
"I hope this will help you."
"Oh, by the way, if you happen to find a ring on your adventures, I lost my favorite gold ring.  I will give a reward to the one who returns it."
"Come back in the morning, please."
"Those fairies are cute, but they play rough!"
"It is shaped in gold like a braid of the herb Althelas with entwined leaves."
"I don't know how I lost it.  I hardly ever take it off."
"You already have your reward."
"I will give six golds to the person who returns my ring.  It was a gift when I graduated from the college of Healing."
"I will pay you for Cheetaur claws, Troll beard, magic mushrooms, and flowers from Erana's Peace."
"I use them to make my potions."
"This potion is used to heal damage and keep wounds from getting worse.  It has gotten pretty dangerous around here, and a Healing Potion can be quite useful in a serious situation."
"Vigor Potion is used to revitalize yourself after vigorous exercise.   It helps to restore stamina."
"Magic Potions restore the energy needed to cast spells."
"I make up potions that heal injuries.  I will be happy to sell you a Healing Potion if you like."
"I make and sell Healing Potions, Magic Potions, Vigor Potions and Undead Unguent."

"The Potions will cost you: 
            Healing Potion 40 silvers 
            Magic Potion 60 silvers 
            Vigor Potion 20 silvers 
            Undead Unguent 100 silvers."
"Undead Unguent is used to drive off the minor undead such as zombies or floating spirits.  It doesn't last long, so you should use it only when you are anticipating an encounter with such things."
"There are not too many zombies in this land... too damp, I suppose.  There are some floating spirits, I have heard."
"Floating Spirits are the ghosts which usually hang around graveyards at night.  They are dangerous, so don't go near them unless you use Undead Unguent first."
"Stamina is a measure of the energy the body uses as you work or play."
"I've never heard of a monster around here with green fur.  Rare things are sometimes by their nature magical."
"Fairy Dust obviously comes from fairies.  They dance around mushroom rings when it is night.  Fairies are magical beings, so be careful around them."
"I use flowers from Erana's Peace to the north in nearly all my potions.  They have some of the magic of the place even when they are dried."
"I will pay you five silvers for a flask full of flowers."
"I use the mushrooms from a fairy ring to make Magic Potions."
"I will pay you one gold for some."

"The Cheetaur looks like a cross between a panther and a man.  It is vicious and tough.  Unless you are a very tough fighter, you had best try to get away from it.  If you do manage to kill it, then I will pay you five silvers for each claw

"The Trolls around here are tough monsters that cannot stand the light of day and so are found at night or in caves.  They are very difficult to kill, but I will pay two healing potions for the beard of a Troll."
"I have heard that the Dryad of the woods knows a Dispel Potion to disenchant people with spells upon them."
"I'm not sure what kind of water that is."
"The only place you can get a magic acorn from is a Dryad's oak tree."
"I will pay you one silver for each empty flask you bring me so I can use it for more potions.  Waste not, want not, I always say."
"Well, my name is Amelia Appleberry, but mostly I am just known as the Healer around here."
"Oh, that's my pet, Pterry, the pterosaur.  He has a girl friend, Pteresa, who has a nest in the oak outside my door.  Pterry keeps me company and listens to my chatter as I work."
"They are a species of flying lizard.  I understand they can grow quite large in the south."
"Poor man, he hasn't been the same since his son and daughter were taken from him."
"He was just a young dashing man when he last rode off and never returned."
"The dear child, I can still picture her with her beautiful blond hair done up in braids.  She was so sweet.  It's hard to believe she's gone."
"Those brutes!  I'm always having to heal someone they've beaten up and robbed.  I hate thieves and brigands."
"Would you believe the brigands almost killed him a while back?  Fortunately their leader made them bring Heinrich here where I could heal him."
"Almost due north of here is the meadow called Erana's Peace.  It is a very magical area and it is a place of safety.  It is beautiful all year, for the flowers are always in bloom."
"I have the skill to use my magic to create potions.  I am a bit proud about it.  Not everyone can do that, you know."
"Mandrake root is used in a variety of spells, mostly for evil purposes.  Mandrake must be pulled from a deadman's grave at midnight.  The root is particularly powerful."
"I'm sorry, I just don't know much about that.  Perhaps you should ask someone else."
"Oh, thank you for finding my ring.  How I've missed this.  Here are six golds and two Healing Potions for your reward!"
Thank you, but I have all the flowers I need.
Thank you. I often use flowers from Erana's Peace in potion making. Here are your silvers.
Thank you, but I have enough mushrooms.
You have ruined the mushrooms with toadstools.  I can not buy your mushrooms.
These are very nice. I'll dry them and grind them into a powder. Let me think...oh yes! I said these are worth a gold. Here you are.

"Do you mean to tell me that you actually managed to kill a Cheetaur?  You're quite a hero, aren't you?  Here is your money.
You might think about purchasing a Healing Potion in case you have to fight such monsters again."
Don't tell me you actually killed a Troll? Why, you are really amazing. I haven't had any Troll's Beard for a long time. Here are two Healing Potions for the beard.
"So, you say this is one of the ingredients for the Dispel Potion.  Well, I had better get started making it."
"Thank you.  I'm sure that I'll find a good use for Fairy Dust."
"I don't need any more Flying Water.  Thank you anyway."
"Thank you for the offer, but I really don't need any water."
"I'm sorry, but this water does not seem to have any magical potential at all.  This can't be the `Flying Water' you described."

characters.name: Baba Yaga
characters.voiceDesc: Calm, clever, and dangerously composed. This female voice drips with sarcasm and intelligence, effortlessly outwitting adversaries. Suited for dark fantasy, sinister animation, and cunning antagonists with an edge of mischief and menace.

lines.text: "Look pets...we have a visitor!"

"Powers of Night,
Shadows of Day
Heed now my Words!
Henceforth you STAY!"
"Well Dearies!  What shall we have for supper today?"

"Hero sandwiches?
I had something more formal in mind."

"Ah...that's it!
Frog Legs Fricassee."
"Now how does that spell go.....?"

"Hear me, oh Powers
Of Klatha and Mana!
Turn now my guest
Into species called Rana!"
"I learned that spell from Erasmus, kids. Doesn't it look delicious?"
"Stubborn, aren't you?"
"Well, you won't be so tough after you've simmered in a little wine sauce."
"Bon appetit, Dearies!"
"It's just as well.  I'd lose my dinner if you were brave."
"Lovies, it's about time we dine."
"Well, if you won't be a sweet, then you'll be my meat!"
"Nothin' says lovin' like frog legs in the oven."
"No mandrake for me, eh?  What a waste of spells you turned out to be."
"Actually, since it's my breakfast time, we'll have some amphibian omelette with bacon and legs."
"You again?"
"Hello, Hero."

"Powers that rule
Over regions soggy:
Change this creature
Back into a froggy!"
"Ooooh...yummy!  I'm glad you returned."

"What have you DONE to me?
How DARE you use my own spell against me?
I'll, I'll....Oh oh!"

"Hear me, Oh Element of Air and Wind.
Give me the power to save my own skin."
"Oops!  I very nearly forgot..."

"Hear me, oh Powers
Of Mana and More:
Turn this fool back
As he was before."
Wheeeeeere's myyyyyy Maaaandraaaaake Rooooooooooot????
"Back so soon?"

"Spirits of Mist
And Creatures of Bog:
Transform my guest
To the shape of a Frog."

"This I vow:
Stay there now!"
"Yum, Yum.  Froggie Frappe!"
"Did you bring me my mandrake like you promised?"
"Well?  Where is it?"
"What's the matter?  Got a frog in your throat?"
"I suppose I'll have to turn you back into whatever it is that you were.  Pity!  You're much more appetizing this way."

"Creatures of Bog
And Spirits of Fog:
Return the true form
To this rather dumb Frog."
"Now, did you put it in your backpack?"
"Ah!  Here it is."
"Kids!  We have it!"
"That's it!  The final ingredient.  Now we can make our greatest creation..."
"MANDRAKE MOUSSE!"
"What's that, children?  You think we should reward our lackey here?"
"Very well, ex-frog.  I'll let you live this time.  Next time, though, it's frog legs for sure!"
"So...Go!"
"BE STILL!"
"Critics!  We can't all be gourmands, I suppose."

Now, Oh-Soon-To-Be-Supper...
I don't suppose you have a name?"
"So you're the one who's trying to be a hero around here.  The only good hero's a dead hero, I always say!"
"But I do have a need for a brave fool.  Are you brave?"
"Wellll..."
"If you're willing to do a small little teensy favor for me, I might reconsider having you for supper."

"I need the root of a mandrake plant
that grows in the graveyard.  Will you be
a sweet and fetch me some?"
"And I had my mouth watering for frog.  Oh well!"

"Hear what I say
And hear me right:
Mandrake must be pulled
At precisely Midnight!"

"This I tell you
And this I say:
Return with the root
Ere the break of next day."

"Hear what I say
And know I don't lie:
Bring back the root
Or else you will die!!"
"You, Shoo!"

characters.name: Bruno
characters.voiceDesc: Eccentric, husky-voiced Northerner with odd takes, sharp instincts, and charming contradictions.

lines.text: "So what's this about, anyway?"
"What's the bee in her bonnet?"
"What's it ta do with us?"
"Maybe we should just make our move now."
"Where's the back door, then?"
"Yeah."
"What Word?"
"Oh yeah, sure."
"What is it?"
"You think I'm a dummy or somethin'?"
"Hey, no problem.  What's the 'word' again?"
"Hiden Goseke.  Got it."
"Hiden Goseke.  See ya."
Ya need ta talk to Sneak about the password. Ya can find him in the alley at night.
"Get outta my face."
"Give me dough- ya know."
"A little silver might loosen my lip."
"What's in it for me?  If ya give me a silver, I might have a bit of info you can use."
"For another silver, I can tell ya about where to go to find the Baron, Baba Yaga, or some goblins who have a lot of treasure. So if ya want some information, hand me some cash."
"Okay, whaddaya wanna know"
"Go south and you'll see a hollow log.  Then head west until ya can go south again.  Then go east until you can go south again.  From there go west. Them brigands just love vistors."
"What's this for?"
"Don't get me wrong.  I'll take it!"
"I seen her one time yelling out to the house, 'Hut of brown, now sit down.'  That's one spooky dame, let me tell you."
"For ten golds, I'll tell ya where to find some of the robbers what have been robbing people coming into the valley."
"They hide it in the graveyard.  It's under one of the tombstones.  You can see the thieves if you go there at night."
"Okay, whaddaya wanna know?"
"For ten golds, I'll even tell ya where to find some of the robbers what have been robbing people coming into the valley."
"Go south and you'll see a hollow log.  Then head west until ya can go south again.  Then go east until you can go south again.  From there, go west.  Them brigands just love visitors."
"For two gold, I'll tell ya how to get into old Baba Yaga's hut."
"Nobody knows much about the leader."
"For ten gold, I'll tell ya where some of those robbers are that're robbin' folks as they come inta the valley."
"Just call me Bruno.  That was an easy silver."
"She's an ugly hag who knows some magic.  She lives in a weird house."
"Her place is due west of the Baron's place.  Can't miss it.  And for two gold, I'll tell ya how to get into her hut."
"Ya just take the road north from here past the Healer's and go till ya get to his castle."
"The little pests are just northwest of the cemetery.  They're a bunch of wimps."
"It's due west of town.  Nice place to visit, but I wouldn't want to stay, if ya know what I mean."
"Everyone loves Dragon's Breath."
"The Sheriff and the goon are pals.   Otto even lives with the Sheriff and his wife."
"He's a pal of mine.  He usually hangs out in the bar.  A real sweet guy.  Give him a slap on the back for me."
"The thieves' password?  Sorry, I can't risk losing my thief license.  Chief wouldn't like it if I blabbed that to the likes'a you."
"He's the only one in the valley who can beat me at daggers."
"She's got some good potions.  Her hut is just to the north of here."
"He's some kinda magic user.  And he laughs alot.  That's all I know."
"Hahahaha!  Yeh, the antwerp is a good monster for you to fight.  It loves to play."
"Lots'a monsters in this valley.  Most of 'em are wimps, though."
"Hey, I just know what I know.  If you want other info, ask someone else."
"For a gold, well, I can tell where the Thieves' Guild is hidden."
"You talk'n ta me?"
"Why dontcha leave me alone?  I ain't done nothin'."
"Hey, I'm just trying to make a buck.  No need to give me a hard time."
"You look nervous.  Why dontcha take a walk and cool off."
"Bug off."
"I think I hear your momma calling you."
"Your boot's untied."

characters.name: Henry the Hermit
characters.voiceDesc: Warm and nostalgic, this voice feels like listening to your grandpa tell bedtime stories by the fireplace. Loving and genuine, perfect for heartfelt narration.

lines.text: "All right, already!"
"Is someone there?"
"You again?  Either come up, or go away.   I really doesn't cares which."
"Oh, 'ello.  Come right up."
"Just climb the ladder."
"Just a minute!  Just a minute!"
"'ello?"
"Well, it's dark and damp and the waters roars sos there's not a moment's quiet.  The ceiling drips and the walls are slimy and it's always cold.  But, oh well, sez I, it's 'ome."

"A 'ermit is one wot lives alone, far away from them wot talks too much or asks too many questions.  'Ermits are shy, quiet types wot don't say much.  Me brother 'Arry 'ardly says a word in a year.  Me sister 'Ortense 'asn't spoken since sh
"Me family lives pretty far apart from each other. We're all 'ermits, you know."

"I'm 'enry.  'Enry the eighth I is.  Me farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry. And
"I likes to play the game.  I can show you how to play, if you likes."
"It's the spell wot sets off uther spells."
"Like when you wants to see the ladder, you casts a trigger and there you 'ave it, large as life."
"Course, you gots to 'ave a spell on the invisible ladder to make it visible first.  But it 'as its uses."
"I thinks I 'ave a scroll with the spell, if you be knowing sum magic, that is."
"The spells wot I nose is pretty simple maybe, but I like 'em."
"I can make me ladder show up and me door close and even get rid o' pests using the trigger spell, you see."
"I nose sum magic.  Erasmus the wizard tot me sum spells."
"He's wot put the spell on me ladder sos the brigands don't get up.  Them wot climbs th' rocks gets a door opening in their nose."
"This 'ere's wot's called the Flying Falls."
"I've doon sum flying falls arund 'ere meself. That porch gets slippery sumtimes.  He, heh, he."
"Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there.  You're welcome to stay the night since you've shared your rations.  When you want to sleep, just say so."
"Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there.  You're welcome to come back and sleep tonight since you've shared your rations."
"Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there. You're welcome to stay if you share some rations and play some cribbage."
"If you be wanting to learn the spell, I'll be pleased to share it to you.  It's just getting molding in me 'iding place."
"I think you be 'avin too little magic for it to be any use to you."
"Do you want it?"
"Oh well, maybe sumone else can use it sumtime."
"You already 'ave me only scroll."
"Yes, I still have me magic scroll, but you didn't seem to want it. 'Ave you changed your mind?"
"Let's see.  'Ow does Erasmus do it?  Oh, yes."
" 'E summons it with a Trigger.  'Ere goes!"
"There you 'as it."
"I don't 'ave much, but wot I 'ave, I 'ave, an' t'isn't for sale."
"Well, see'en as I don't get out much, I don't know much."
"If you be ever needen a place to stay the night and you're in the area, I could be willen to put you up for the night for sum rations and a couple o' games o' cribbage."
"You can't sleep here, you got's no rations to give me."
"E's me friend, 'e and Fenrus.  Powerful wizard 'e is."
"I 'ere 'is 'ouse is sumthin' to see up north."
"I don't make it out much, and 'e's pretty busy, sos I don't see 'em too often.  But when we do all get together, you can be sure we'll 'ave fun."
" 'E loves to play 'is games, 'e do."
"Fenrus is Erasmus' familiar or is that the other way around?"
"Erasmus' favorite game is the Mage's Maze.  'Ave to be a magic user to play it, though.  Erasmus is awful good at it."
"Nasty sorts, they is.  I sees them sneaking arund the woods all the times.  Uther then the Warlock, I 'as nothing to do with them"
"E's not so bad.  Got a good sense o' 'umor, 'e 'as. I get the giggles just to think of 'im.  He heh he heh he."
" 'E's come by at times to chat.  Borrowed the mirror wot I borrowed from Erasmus 'e did."
"A magic mirror of reflection, it was.  If you use it, when a nasty spell was cast at you, it was wot sent it back at the one wot cast it.  Do un to others, I sez.  Heh, he, he."
"Come in, come in.  Good to see you again.   Get's kind o' tiresome sitten here an' thinken by meself all the time."
"Ello.  'Ow are you?  'Ave we met before?"
"I'm 'Enry the 'ermit, that's me.  Me Farther was an 'ermit and me Murther was an 'ermit sos I come by me job rightly."
"Don't 'ave too many visitors.  'Ermits don't, you know.  (Part o' the job description.) I likes to see a new face, though."
"Good to 'ear anurther's speaking besides meself.  Sos wot can I do for you?"

characters.name: Zara the Magic Shop Owner
characters.voiceDesc: A female voice like dark velvet—low, sultry, and subtly dangerous. Ideal for villainous or seductive characters in video games, or to add intrigue and allure to YouTube voiceovers.

lines.text: "The powers of magic are reserved for those with the potential to use them.  I sense no such potential in you."
"May this aid you on your Quest!"
I detect that you have not the money to purchase that item.  Do not toy with me!
"My name is Zara.  This is my shop."
"I am both human and Faery Folk.  I draw my Power from both."
"We are a people of Power and Magic, and live in the forests far beyond the mountains to the west."
"Monsters stay out of town, and I stay in.  They are of no concern to me."
"Damiano is my familiar and my companion.  We share our lives and our magic."
"When you have mastered nine spells, have the power to cast them all, and proved yourself worthy by accomplishing a great deed, then you must undergo initiation to become a full Wizard."
"There is a place in the distant south where you must journey, but first you must become a hero here."
"A Wizard is one who both shapes Magic and is shaped by it.  Erasmus and I are the only Wizards in this valley."
"There is much Magic in this world for those who know how to use it.  There is Magic in this little town, and a good deal of Magic in this valley."
"What I sell here are merely tools to help those who have power.  You may purchase restorative potions and scrolls from which you may learn spells if you have the potential."
"There is an Aura protecting this town from danger.  Within most of the town's walls, there can be no act of violence or cruel Magic.  Even so, it is prudent to avoid dark places."
"An Aura is a Spell of protection surrounding something.  The town is still protected by the Aura cast by the great Spellcaster, Erana."

"There is much Power in this valley, and it attracts those who use Magic.  I am here in Spielburg, Erasmus has his house on Zauberberg, and even Baba Yaga has her hut cooped up somewhere around here."
"She is a powerful and wicked hag.  You would be wise to avoid her.  She cursed the Baron von Spielburg when he tried to drive her away."
"This is Baba Yaga's curse, cast upon the Baron years ago:"
"Upon von Spielburg and all his clan, this Curse I now demand:  What I will shall come full measure, so shall ye lose all that ye treasure."
"There is always a way to break a curse; possibly Erasmus knows more about this one."
"Baba Yaga's hut is Magical.  It stands on chicken legs, and you must know the rhyme to enter."
"I have no interest in entering Baba Yaga's hut and therefore do not know the rhyme."
"He was once the great leader of this land until he was foolish enough to anger Baba Yaga."
"Erana was a powerful Spellcaster who lived long ago.  She brought peace to this valley.  Even now her spell protects this town from violence or foul Magic."
"Her final resting place is due north of town, and it is a place of both safety and healing.  It is known as `Erana's Peace'"
"Erasmus is a Wizard and a Spellcaster who knows much about this area.  He lives in a tower eastward of town.  He can be very helpful if approached properly, but he has a strange sense of humor."
"It is a steep mountain to the northeast."
"That is the Spell that lets you cast a Magical Flame upon your enemy.  The Spell Scroll will cost you 60 silver."
"That is the Spell that lets you place magical energy upon a weapon.  The energy will be released as magical damage when you hit your enemy."
"It is a Spell that allows you at first to unfasten simple locks and knots.  As you gain in skill, then you may open doors with it.  This Spell Scroll will cost you 30 silver."
"The Fetch Spell allows you to lift or lower a lightweight object from a distance.  The Spell Scroll will cost you 40 silver."
"We sell several Magical Spells on study scrolls.  You may purchase Flame Dart for 60 silver, Fetch for 40 silver, or the Open spell for 30 silver."
"The Scrolls we sell are Magical.  You have but to read the Spell, and you will learn it."
"I also know that you can find a wonderful Spell if you learn the secret of Erana's Peace."
"Secrets are made to be kept."
"We can sell you spell learning scrolls and enchanted potions."
"The Healing Potion will cost you 50 silver.  It is made by the Healer outside of town."
"Vigor Potions restore your Stamina after you have exerted yourself.  They cost 25 silver each."
"Power is the essence of magic, that which the wizard shapes and is shaped by."
"The Power Potion restores your magical energy, and costs 75 silver."
"We have Healing, Vigor, and Power Potions."
"Master the Arts Arcane, use those skills to vanquish the evil curse, and you will become a true Hero!"
"We are entities of Magic and Power.  Matters such as that are of little importance."
"We choose only to answer questions which interest us.  Do not persist with uninteresting questions."
"We shall not waste our time further."
"You may choose from the Fetch, Open, or Flame Dart Spells."
"You may choose from the Healing, Vigor, or Power Potions."
"I do not sell such things to such as you."
"So, you return to my shop.  Ask of me what you will, but keep to the point."
"I am Zara, and my companion is Damiano."
"The items in this shop are designed for those skilled in the use of magic.  We have very little for ones such as you who have not been initiated, but what we have could prove useful."

characters.name: Wolfgang Abenteuer
characters.voiceDesc: Charles Haversham is a distinguished British male voice with a deep, commanding tone and impeccable diction. A natural storyteller with a refined presence, he is ideal for audiobooks, fiction, documentaries, and sophisticated editorial content. His voice blends gravitas, warmth, and theatrical finesse, making every phrase feel deliberate, articulate, and engaging.

lines.text: "Oh, my young friend!  What shall we talk about?  I have so many stories.  Just ask me about something."
"Ja.  What with the baron losing his son and daughter, all the monsters, Baba Yaga, and now the brigands, it has been one thing after another."

"Baron von Spielburg was once a brave protector of our valley.  We used to adventure together when we were younger.  No brigand or monster would dare show his face here if the Baron had not angered Baba Yaga.  Now it is said that he goes no

"He was a hero and worthy of the name Barnard von Spielburg.  He rode off to hunt one morning five years ago, and his horse returned with large and deep claw marks.  No sign of the Baronet's body was ever found."
"Elsa was a beautiful eight-year-old child with blonde hair and sky-blue eyes.  She was the Baron's pride and joy."
"Ten years ago she was carried off by something which came over the wall and flew off with her.  The search for her lasted many years, but at last everyone gave up."
"Everyone, that is, except for the Baron's Jester, Yorick."
"Yorick was a funny little man, but brave.  He swore he would spend his life searching for the Baron's Elsa."
"She is the center of our problems, I think.  Baba Yaga is a powerful Ogress.  Baron von Spielburg tried to force her to leave this valley, but she cursed him."
"Now the Baron has lost everything but his land, and I don't know how long he will keep that.  What our valley needs is a Hero."

"Did I ever tell you about the days when Schultz and I rid this valley of Antwerps?  Ja, we were real adventurers then, and this was a true Guild Hall.  Now we are just old men, and this is just a place to tell old stories."
"Schultz and I have been friends for a long time.  He too has been a real adventurer.  Now, he is just the Sheriff of the town."
"Even a thief can be a hero sometimes, as long as uses his skills only for good purposes.  But most thieves are far from being heroes."
"This is, of course, where an adventurer can find out where there is a need for someone brave and courageous.  There are jobs on the bulletin board over there."

"It is also a good place to talk about adventures on a cold afternoon.  We used to play cards here once a week, as well, but there are too few adventurers in Spielburg anymore.  They all died at the hands of monsters or brigands, or they ju
"You can see some of the types of monsters that live around here if you look at our walls."
"There is a reward for anyone who can stop the brigands by capturing or killing their leaders.  The information is on the bulletin board."
"Oh, those are nothing.  Anyone can kill a Saurus."
"It's been a while since Schultz and I killed the one on the wall.  It is fortunate that few Trolls remain; they're deadly."
"Watch out for Cheetaurs.  I bear the scars of my last fight with one to this day."
"I remember the day I killed one.  It was the biggest one I had ever seen.  It put up a tough fight, but I was tougher.  You can see the result on the wall."
"One day, years ago, a pair of dragons tried to take over our valley.  We adventurers rode out to meet them."
"I can still see Stefan von Spielburg charging forward on his black horse.  He slew the one whose head adorns the wall above our fireplace.  The other one flew off."
"People say that they can sometimes see that other dragon flying high overhead, but it has never dared attack us again."

"One year, this valley was overrun by those odd and terrible monsters.  Schultz and I fought long and hard to eliminate them completely.  We might have failed even so, had it not been for those two peculiar tourists who came to our aid."
"That was the most vicious moose I ever ran into.  Nearly bit my nose off!"
"They certainly were strange."
"That's where the tourists said they came from.  Must be some place up North -- I sure never heard of it."
"Not much of an adventurer, but he did kill one Saurus before he made the mistake of tangling with an Ogre."
"Ogres are a lot like Goons, but even meaner.  Not as bright, though."
"A Goon is a lot like an Ogre, not as tough, but a little smarter.  We have two Goons in town, Otto and Crusher."
"I am Wolfgang Abenteuer, Guild Master of this hall."
"A real hero is someone who did not start out strong or powerful, but who uses his courage, brains, and skills to become the best he can be."
"I'm sorry.  I only know what's written on the poster."
"The castle is just north of the Healer's house."
"The Healer's house is just north of the crossroads outside of town."
"You know, that reminds me very little of the time old Schultz and I......."
"Wait a minute. It'll come to me."
"ZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZ."
"Ach! I was so busy I didn't notice you come in. Welcome, welcome! It is so seldom that we have new adventurers here. Most people think this valley is cursed!"
"Welcome back! It is good to see you again. So many adventurers never return. Someday, you and I will go on an adventure together."
"Hrrmph! Ahem! Yes, as I was saying....where were we?"
"Oh, what a glorious day it was! I...... Oh, hello there!"
"You fiendish witch! I will have you...... Oh, it's you, my friend!"
"Oh, Barnard, bravest of the brave, you cannot be gone from us so soon.... What? What? You're still here, my fellow adventurer?"

characters.name: Bonehead (gate skull)
characters.voiceDesc: A classic FM disc jockey voice inspired by 1950s–1980s American radio. Energetic, nostalgic, and playful—ideal for animation, video games, and comedy content. The “Hey, it’s Brad!” vibe brings retro Top 40, rock, or oldies flair to any project.

lines.text: "All the other skulls have glowing eyes. But me?....noooo!  I don't need lovely glowing eyes!  Just open and close the gate, that's all I'm good for."
"But if you can give me a glowing gem for my eyes, then I'll let you in the gate."
"Well, is it a deal or isn't it?"
"You know the deal!  Get me a glowing gem for my eyes, and I'll let you in."
"Do you agree to the deal?"
"I'm moving as fast as I can!"
"The deal is made, then.  A glowing gem in exchange for an opportunity to see Baba Yaga herself."
"Give me my gem!!"
"Hey, it's your life!"
"No gem, no entry.  That's the deal."
"What kind of hero are you?  Can't even find a little glowing gem for me to see with!"
"Just answer the question, will ya?"
"Do you have my gem?"
"Do you want back in?"
"Is it a deal or not?"
"Right!"
"Not until you get my gem!"
"Hey!  Where's your manners?  Let me tell you something, here!"
"Wait a minute...I'll get around to that."
"You kept your end of the deal, all right!"
"What's to tell?  You've seen her for yourself."
"Baba Yaga is the most powerful Ogress around.  If you have any brains (and it looks like you don't), you'll stay away from her."
"Some hero YOU are!  Don't know what an Ogre is.  Just check out Baba Yaga!"
"All I ask for is a little gem or jewel that glows in the dark.   You're the big hero, so go find one."
"It's just the right color!"
"I wish I had some."
"I'm thrilled to have some!"
"The bone brains on top of the fence are Baba Yaga's spies.  That's why THEY have glowing eyes."
"It's not much, but I call it home."
"You've seen one, you've seen them all, I say."
"Of course, most fences aren't poisoned on top!"
"Baba Yaga's hut will squat down if you say the rhyme."
"The rhyme is:  'Hut of brown, now sit down'."
"My time is important, and I'm very busy, as you can well see.  Ask me about something important."
"I can see!  I have eyes again!!"
"Yeccchhh!  Is THAT what you look like???"
"Oh well!  Have fun visiting Baba Yaga.  And good luck....you'll need it!"
"Baba Yaga does not welcome strangers!  You have to deal with me before you can enter."
"Baba Yaga is one tough magic user.  If you're so stupid that you want to enter, perhaps we can make a deal."
"So you're back!  Changed your mind about making a little deal, hero?"
"Did you get it?  Did you get my glowing gem???"
"So you made it back, did you?  She is expecting you."
"You again??  Do you really want to go back in there?"
"I hope you can remember the rhyme."

characters.name: Chief Thief
characters.voiceDesc: Smug and Theatrical, this middle-aged male voice is ideal for villainous characters. It also brings elements of Charismatic, Dramatic and conveys emotions like Wicked, Confident. Great for formats such as video games, anime. Perfect for users needing a voice that blends character with clarity.

lines.text: "How much will you bet?"
"I don't have time to talk to unlicensed scum.  Pay your guild license fee, and then we can talk."
"What do you think this is, your home?  Crusher, deal with this deadbeat."
"I have no time for idle talk.  Why don't you do something constructive like stealing Otto's yo-yo?"
"A dagger is a thief's best friend, and throwing from a distance is the safest and best way to use it.  If you think you are good enough, perhaps we could play a little game."
"We use the specially designed Dag-Nab-It board you can see there on the wall."
"We could start small, perhaps five silvers a game, perhaps even less.  Of course, we can wager more if you think you can take me."
"There are only a few members of our Guild still around here.  With the snow and the brigands keeping away the tourists, it's hard to earn a decent living."
"Where else but here can one mingle freely with others of our chosen profession, sharing tips and secrets?  Where else can you safely fence stolen items?"
"Where else can you ask questions like these and not get thrown into jail or attacked?  The Guild is truly your home away from home."
"Those who do not pay their union dues are the lowest form of life."
"My advice is, don't get caught."
"Now that would be telling."
"Just give your stolen items to Boris and you will be suitably recompensed.  Or, in terms more suited to your apparent intellect, you get cash for your loot."
"Boris is our accountant."
"Crusher is our personnel manager."
"Otto is the Sheriff's goon.  He shouldn't give you any trouble unless you happen to stand in his way."
"The Sheriff provides a useful service in this town.  He arrests anyone too stupid or unlucky to be a good thief."
"I am known as 'The Chief'.  But you can call me 'Chief'."
"These non-union cretins are giving thieves a bad name.  I'm sure they have a spy planted in our organization, but I don't know who it is."
"There is certainly a traitor in our midst."
"There are a few places around town where a clever and skilled thief can make some silver.  Perhaps even you could steal something."

"I have had beginners before, but this Guild reached a new low when you joined.  Child, you are a thief.  Stealing is how you make a living.  So go out there and steal something and hopefully you'll get, umm, that is, you *won't* get caught

"We will gladly sell you a lock pick for a modest fee.  However, for the true professional, nothing will do but the Guild-Approved Acme Thief Tool Kit.  This kit will allow you to pick anything with a little practice, and is a real steal at
"Now that has to be one of the stupidest things anyone has ever asked me."
"Beginners!  Why do they keep giving me beginners?"
"I tell you, Crusher, that a thief of my stature and abilities should be stuck in a podunk town besieged by brigands who aren't even Union Members is the height of injustice!"
"It will cost you 25 silvers to work in town.  Just pay Boris at the door.  If you don't have the cash, go steal something."
"Let's finish this game first."
"If you don't bet, you can't play."
"Now, let's not get carried away."
"Hey!  This is cash on the table.  You can't bet what you don't have."
"So, you think you're pretty hot stuff, eh?  Well, we'll see about that."
"You got me that time.  Say, you're pretty tough!"
"Looks like I won that one.  Must have gotten lucky."
"It's a tie.  Let's double the wager."
"Hey, nice game.  It's a tie."
Shall we play again?
"Just answer the question.  Shall we play again?"
"Abandon mope, all ye who enter here!"
"Job hunting, or just sight-seeing?"
"I foresee a brilliant future for you in the fall."
"If you've got something to say, then speak fast or forever hold your pieces."
"State your case before I case your estate!"

characters.name: The Brigand Warlock
characters.voiceDesc: A measured, elderly male voice with subtle menace. Slightly eccentric, calm yet unpredictable, and psychologically complex. This voice creates intrigue through stillness and unpredictability—perfect for enigmatic or morally ambiguous roles.

lines.text: "No more talking, my friend.  Let's have fun!"
"You know me?  Then perhaps you have come to help.  Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band."
"Be more specific, my friend."
"Talking is a speciality of those who know not what they talk about."
"You know Elsa?  Then perhaps you have come to help.  Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band."
"It's about time that someone else helped Elsa before it's too late!  Toro and I are the only friends she's got here."
"She's a lot like her dad, though.  She organized these brigands and changed them from mere thugs to major raiders!"
"She plans to move the entire band away from the valley as soon as the snow clears.  If that happens, I know she will never be disenchanted."
"You know about the enchantment?  Then perhaps you have come to help.  Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band."
"Elsa doesn't know who she really is.  That's part of the enchantment."
"When Elsa was left with some brigands ten years ago, all her memories were taken away from her."
"I borrowed a Magic Mirror from a friend to try to break the spell, but it only works when spells are cast directly at you."
"If you're going to be the big Hero, I hope you brought a Dispel Potion or something."
"Alas, poor Yorick!  I know me well."
"Ten years ago, I swore I would find Elsa no matter what.  It took me two years to find her, here with the brigands, and I've posed as her warlock ever since."
"I couldn't break the spell on her, nor could I leave her to grow up into an attractive woman surrounded by rogues and scoundrels."
"Someone had to defend her honor!"
"Can't you see that I am he?  I try to inspire (before they expire) a sense of awe and majesty in those that enter here."
"After all, I am the Brigand Warlock.  One has to keep up one's appearances, or appear to keep up with one's peers."
"Everybody needs to know a few magic tricks to get by these days.  You'd be surprised how effective Sleeping Powder is for taking out the guards of a caravan."
"You snooze, you lose.  And my banana trick has appeal of its own."
"For thirty years I was the Court Jester.  Now I jest enjoy watching other people take the pratfalls."
"I'll protect the Brigand Leader with everything I've got.  That's no laughing matter as I get madder."
"The true Dispel Potion will break an enchantment.  You must splash the potion on Elsa as soon as you can, or she'll skewer you for sure.  She's more than a match for old "Swordy Lordy" now!"
"Of which potion do you speak?"
"You must know the "Blade Braggart".  He used to show off in the castle courtyard all the time."
"Elsa once asked if "Rapier Ruler" would teach her to use the sword, and the "Sword Bore" told her he would not waste his talent on females."
"She came to me in tears.  After all, she was only nine at the time."
"She can teach old "Weapon Windbag" a thing or two now!  Her arm is true, and she's truly disarming."
"There are plenty of doors.  You wouldn't want me to spoil your fun, now!"
"Either you'll figure it out in the end, or you'll end it before you're out."
"This room is my way of keeping the brigands from bothering the Leader about raises, since it tends to raze the brigands."
"I designed it myself!  This place has people rolling in the aisles."
"Ah yes, the "Mirror with the Leer", as Erasmus calls it (or was it Fenrus?).  It reflects a spell back on the caster of the spell."
Now where did I put that mirror... was it on the desk in Elsa's office?
"Toro is the minotaur that guards this fortress.  Elsa found him when he was just a calf, and he's kowtowed to her ever since."
He guarantees that the brigands won't give Elsa any bull."
"This fort is the brigand's palace.  It has been well-constructed."
"Would he be surprised to see his little girl now!"
"If you have business to do, then get busy doing your business."
"Can't you see that's nothing to me?  You'd better flee."
"The brigands will come to spoil your fun.  You'll be undone."
"The brigands tend to hold a grudge, and they won't begrudge to tend to you."
"If you have any famous last statements, you'd better say the secret word, or you'll lament your last state."

characters.name: Baron Stefan von Spielburg
characters.voiceDesc: Deep, rich, warm, and confident. Smooth and reassuring with quiet strength—great for podcasts, advertising, sales, and narration.

lines.text: "Words cannot express the gratitude I have towards you."
"Not only have you freed the Baronet from enchantment and allowed my son to return to me, but you have renewed my hope that the curse may be lifted."
"My son, Baronet Barnard von Spielburg, also desires to acknowledge his indebtedness."
"Barnard."
"Furthermore, I understand you have rid our valley of a dangerous Kobold Magic User in order to free the Baronet von Spielburg."
"You risked your life to singlehandedly defeat the foul Spellcaster.  You are a true hero, and I thank you."
"I sincerely hope you will continue on your valiant adventures in our land.  If anyone can rid the land of the brigands, it will be you."
"Someday in the near future, I can envision holding a ceremony in your honor and bestowing upon you the title, 'Hero of the Land of Spielburg'."
"It would be nice if someone could finally defeat the brigands and claim the title."
"As you leave the castle tomorrow, you will receive the reward money I long ago posted for the safe return of my son, as a token of my thanks."
"You will, of course, be expected to dine with us and be our guest in the castle tonight."
"Do you have any questions?"
"Just ask about anything you wish to know."
"I see that it is getting late."
"Some years back, I tried to force the Ogress Baba Yaga to leave our valley, and she put a curse upon me that I should lose everything I held dear."
"When I lost my daughter and my son, I lost all interest in living.  My lands have suffered because I suffered."
"Now, though, with the return of my son, I trust the prophecy may yet come true, thanks to you."
"You entered here from the East, and you released my son from the form of a bear.  I am certain you can fulfill the rest of it, and remove this dreadful curse."
"I have attempted everything in my power to get her to go away.  I fear it will take some sort of magic to be able to counteract her evil magic."
"I know very little of magic, much to my sorrow.  Perhaps you should talk to the wizards -- Zara in the town, or Erasmus on Zauberberg."
"Zauberberg, the 'magic mountain', is the home of Erasmus, a kindly, but rather eccentric, wizard.  Zauberberg is directly east of here."
"My beautiful little daughter was stolen away ten years ago by Baba Yaga's magic."
"She has never been found, despite all the searching that has been done for her."
"I thought she was lost forever, but you have renewed my hope that I will again have her beside me."
"My wife has been gone for many years."
"She died giving birth to Elsa, my daughter."
"If I had more guards, I would wipe the brigands out myself, now that I have my son at my side."
"It will take some time before this castle and land have recovered from my sorrow, so I still think it will take a Hero to destroy the brigands."
"I know they have some sort of fortress at the far south of the valley.  I have heard rumors they have set up an ambush for all that approach."
"It is also rumored that there is another way into their fortress, but I have no further knowledge about it."
"Yorick was my court jester, a man of infinite jest.  He went off to find my daughter, but alas, poor Yorick never returned."
"I have offered substantial rewards for the return of my son and my daughter, as well as for capturing or killing the leader and warlock of the brigands."
"There have always been brigands around, but until their new leader and the warlock showed up a few years ago, they were not much of a problem."
"The leader seems to be a brilliant strategist who manages each raid with little loss.  Once their leader is gone, I am sure the brigands can be easily defeated."
"As if this valley hasn't been plagued with enough evil magic users!  From what I have heard, his magic isn't powerful, but he uses it effectively."
"This castle has been in the family for two centuries now.  It was a gift from King Siegfried the Third."
"Now that my son is back, I have much to do.  Kindly restrict your questions to matters of import."
"That is of no importance to your quest, and time is short."
"A servant is awaiting you outside to show you to your quarters.  We will see you at supper later."

characters.name: Fenrus
characters.voiceDesc: A warm, expressive voice tailored for heartfelt oddballs, shy leads, lovable nerds, and socially awkward sidekicks. Perfect for animation, indie games, and podcasts, bringing humor, vulnerability, and emotional nuance to characters who overthink, overshare, and quietly win hearts.

lines.text: I figured one was a dying lizard and the other was a flying wizard.
That one usually floors me, but I'm going to brush it aside before I'm swept away with laughter.
(Gag!)
You're just jealous because the only thing anyone named after you was a soft drink.
Pretty shoddy work, if you ask me!
Just because she never laughs at your jokes doesn't mean that she lacks a sense of humor.
It just means that she has good taste.
So do you!
It's a shame she still beats you!
Nothing reflects well upon that Ogress.  She has a face that would break all but a Magic Mirror.
It's hard to take you seriously when you wear that silly hat!
That means he actually laughs at Erasmus' jokes.
But 'e 'ardly ever 'appens to 'ave much to say.
Reminds me of the one in the master bedroom.
No, but it does show a strange face.  Have you looked in it lately?
It was the flagon with the dragon.  The mirror had a leer.
You wouldn't want to lose your amateur standing, after all.
Does that mean you tease pickles?
That's because his jokes are funnier than yours.
Isn't that like the vampire who likes to take necks out for a good time?
Does that mean a necromancer has ghoul friends?
Canasta with cadavers?
The quote was "Music", not "Magic"!  And it was "food", not "lute"!
"MONEY makes the world go 'round"!
Actually, it was Merlin who said that first, but he used the term "Magician", not "Wizard".
Why is that?
Neither can most rats.
That's because I always win.
Your keys are in the study under the skull.
They're skeleton keys, aren't they?
Like uppity familiars?
Besides, my place is above your reach.
Now that's a spell with charm!
Tell our hero here what happens when you forget and try to open the chest yourself.
Cheese, please!
Ahhhhhh!
"Those rooms are forbidden!"
"Since you're leaving anyway..."
"That'll learn ya!"

characters.name: Weapon Master
characters.voiceDesc: High-energy and expressive, this young to middle-aged medieval-style voice has a higher pitch, ideal for games and animations. Perfect for characters like peasants, grunts, villagers, town criers, and farmers.

lines.text: "Even as we fought, I could detect your skills improving through practice."
"It was most prudent that you should give in to my superior skill."
"Still, you seem to tire more easily than you should."
"Be more aware of the angle with which your weapon approaches your opponent.  The thrust bites quick and deep, but the swing will do more damage if it connects."
"The art of dancing would be a good skill for you to practice, my friend.  The balance of the body over the rapidly moving feet has won many a contest."
"You have a tendency to overbalance when you dodge a blow.  This will adversely affect the timing and effectiveness of your counterattack."
"Put your attention to the control of your legs when you duck a swing.  Your springiness makes you seem to bounce, and a good opponent (like myself) will always take advantage of the opening."

"If you would like a stretcher, my weak, tired friend...
You seem a shadow of the person who began this little lesson."
"Should we meet again, I would not be adverse to another go-round.  Farewell, friend!"
"I salute you!"
En garde!
"Pity.  I'm in the mood for some good combat practice."
"I am a skilled teacher as well as a skilled fighter."
"However, the combat style I teach relies on the subtle alternation of Strike and Parry.  As I observe that you have no skill in the use of Parry, you would receive no benefit from my lessons."
"For a mere gold coin, I will be glad to give you some practice and some pointers on how to better your skills."
"Will you pay for a lesson?"
"Do not ramble, my friend"
"Will you pay?"
"Will you fight?"
"I'm sorry.  Since you have no skill in parrying blows, you would receive no benefit from my instruction."
"Please don't bore me, young adventurer.  Ask me about something interesting to me."
"Fighting is both an art and a skill."
"It is a skill in the use of the muscle strength and agility's control."
"It is an art in the out-witting and out-maneuvering of the opponent."
"I am, of course, a most skilled artist."
"Do you fight?"
"If you were a swordsman skilled in both attack and parry, we could have had a practice bout."
"The sword is the finest of all weapons.  It requires an equal measure of skill and strength."
"I am the Weapon Master."
"You will find them very skilled at fighting.  After all, I am the one who trains all of the guards at this castle."
"The Baron had the wisdom to recognize greatness when I presented myself.  Thus he made me Weapon Master."
"The Baronet had some minor talent with weapons, but he was too impatient.  If he had but listened and learned what I teach, he would never have been lost."
"I never use a shield or armor under most circumstances.  It only gets in the way of my agility and dodging ability.  It is best used by those who are strong, but not swift or agile."
"Practice is the way to refine and improve one's skills."
"If you are addressing me, sir, you must respect me enough to address me to my face!"
Enough!
"I can see now that my career must end.  Anytime that I, the Weapon Master, can be beaten by a mindless, nasty little twerp such as you, it is time to take an interminable vacation."
"By your leave, I hope your sword rusts and your cape fragments in the most awful manner!"

characters.name: The Dryad
characters.voiceDesc: An androgynous, poetic storyteller voice that blends theatrical depth with cinematic rhythm.

lines.text: "Until you have done so, you are only intruding on my concentration."
"You need to become a friend of the forest.  Harm not the gentle creatures or plants. See with all your senses the magic of the trees and wild places."
"Wander and learn.  Then you too will be in harmony with the forest."
"Give the seed to me."
It's not nice to fool Mother Nature.
"Then you shall aid me, and I shall aid you in your quest."
"Bring me a seed from the Spore Spitting Spirea of the North that I may plant it elsewhere in order to preserve these rare and magical plants."
"Thus will you become a true friend of the forest."
"I detect that you have in your possession such a seed.  Are you willing to give me the seed?"
"Please.  Just answer my question."
"Perhaps the farmer has dates in his field."
"I beg your pardon?"
"Have you brought the seed I requested?"
"Well, Hero-to-be!  Are you yet a friend of the woods?"
"I am the Dryad, keeper of the woods.  Are you one with the woods?"
"Now this will boldly grow where none has grown before!"
"Heed now my words, friend of the forest, and heed them well."
"Friend, you must know that there is an evil in this valley which perverts the ways of nature.  The prophecy says that a hero will bring a young human from out this darkness."
"If you are the one to accomplish this task, there is a potion to break enchantments which you must have the Healer make."
"You must gather these ingredients:"
"Flowers from Erana's Peace,"
"Green Fur,"
"Fairy Dust,"
"a Magic Acorn,"
"and Flying Water."
"Farewell, friend.  I must return to my concentration.  May the forest forever surround you."
"You did WHAT???"
"You have destroyed the plant that was to be preserved!"
"As you have destroyed, so shall you now become!"
"You have attempted to harm a free creature of the forest!"
"You shall now become a part of the forest!"
"You have misused your skills, oh Magician!  You have used the Flame Dart on my forest."
"You must be made less dangerous."
"You dare to harm the Dryad of this forest!  You must be made less dangerous."

characters.name: Shameen
characters.voiceDesc: Gentle, warm middle-aged American voice, soothing and relaxing. Perfect for all types of narration, especially ASMR and guided visualization projects.

lines.text: "Welcome!  Welcome, traveler, to the Hero's Tale Inn!  I am Shameen, at your service.  May you find what you seek here."
"May you dream of all the rewards you deserve."
"Very good food, very good drink, finest in town.  Sit, rest, you will be served by my Shema."
"The tavern is located in the northwest corner of our town."
"The Guild Hall is just down the street from here."
"Zara's magic shop is next door to my Inn.  You can't miss it."
"I have run this inn for three years now, with Shema."
"We had thought to return to Shapeir, my homeland, with Abdulla Doo, but Alas, Fate has decreed otherwise."
"Very nice room.  Very soft beds, finest in town.  Only five silvers a night."
I have little knowledge of these things.  Perhaps if you shop around..."
"Poor Abdulla sits.  He tries to forget his loss in food and drink. I think he will not be sorry if you join him at his table."
"My friend, Abdulla, is recovering from his robbery last week.  He should be here around suppertime."
"The world is filled with many kinds of creatures, both good and bad."
"Although in this valley I now have friends, I miss my homeland terribly."
"My apologies, Wanderer, but for such drink you must go to the tavern at the other end of town."
"Alas, the brigands stole all of Abdulla's wealth and mine own when they robbed his caravan.  Now I am forced to stay in this wet, cold land."

"I hoped to return to my homeland with the profits Abdulla, Shema, and I would make from the caravan.  Now, I fear, we will remain here unless someone defeats the brigands and returns our stolen treasures."
"Hot spice.  Hot!  I hope you like!"
"We live far to the south in the desert lands.  Someday, we may yet return there."
"Work of an adventurous sort is available.  You must go to the Hall of the Adventurer's Guild."
"I know little about them except that they must be wealthy now."
"I am Shameen, mate of Shema, innkeeper to the town of Spielburg."
"Shema is my mate and the light of my life. If you sit down, she will be happy to serve you."
We Katta are a race of desert dwellers, wanderers of the endless sands."
"I am but a poor unfortunate, forced to seek my fortune in this cold land, far from my homeland, Shapeir, and my people."
"We of the Katta know only low and simple spells."
"Alas, this humble innkeeper is ignorant of that about which you ask."
"A thousand pardons!  I am a fool and have no answer for your question."
"Forgive me, that I am unable to speak with knowledge on that subject."

characters.name: Sheriff Schultz Meistersson
characters.voiceDesc: Warm and engaging grandpa voice. Ideal for telling adventurous stories and captivating tales to the audience.

lines.text: Just a friendly suggestion, but whatever you do, don't drink the Dragon's Breath.  Not even Otto can drink Dragon's Breath!
"Welcome to our town. You are lucky to have made it down from the mountains before the snow blocked the pass again. It's gotten pretty dangerous outside of town, I understand."
"Many monsters have been trapped around here with the late snow. Between them and the brigands, we certainly could use a Hero around here."
"I am Sheriff Schultz Meistersson. This is Otto Von Goon, my assistant. What do you call yourself? "
"Just walk around and find out for yourself."
"You'll need to talk to the Baron about that. Or go ask Wolfgang down at the Guild Hall, which is just down the street."
"So maybe you're going to be a hero? Well, we could surely use one around here, what with all the brigands."
"There's a whole band of brigands hiding out somewhere in the mountains around us. They robbed a merchant just last week. Got a fair amount of treasure, I hear."
"I am Sheriff Schultz Meistersson, and this is Otto Von Goon, my assistant."
Why, that's me!
"The one who was robbed? He's staying at the Inn, I believe."
"Go talk to the Innkeeper. He will help you there."
"You should know. You came down from there."
My friend, this world's filled with dangers. Sometimes I think it might be better to choose your danger, rather than sitting around, waiting for it, as I seem to do.
"But enough of this philosophical rambling. I am content enough to be Sheriff of Spielburg."
"It's simple. When somone breaks the law, and we catch them, we hold them prisoner."
"Obtaining treasure involves putting yourself in danger. If you're brave, foolish, or lucky, you might get some treasure."
"The monsters come down from the mountains every winter, but they're usually gone by now. The Baron just doesn't have enough men to handle them."
"Wolfgang is the one to talk to about monsters. He's had a lot of experience with them."
"Baron Stefan keeps mostly to himself at his castle north of here. We haven't seen him here in town for years."
"Wolfgang, down at the Guild hall, is the one to talk to about monsters. He's had a lot of experience with them."
Otto is a big help to me. He's very smart for a Goon. His prisoners only suffer a few broken bones when he grabs them.
"It took a bit of doing to teach him to bring prisoners back alive, but he hardly ever forgets, now."
"The Guild Hall is at the Southwest corner of town. If you're looking for work, that's a good place to go."
"Just next to the Inn is a small Magic Shop. The owner, Zara, will let you in if you have some abilities in that area. She's a strange one, all right!"
You think I'm going to tell you everything I know about everything? Go look around and find things out for yourself.
"As safe as our streets are, you still should not sleep on them.  It is a good way to get robbed."
Otto, stop playing with that music box and GO TO BED!
Otto!

characters.name: Shopkeeper
characters.voiceDesc: Warm and trustworthy Irish male voice, ideal for educational and informational videos.

lines.text: "You won't be needing your old leather armor anymore.  I shall give you a 50 silver trade-in discount."
"Here you are; thank you for your patronage."
Unfortunately, I have only standard weapons and equipment. I carry daggers and chain armor. Maybe someday I'll be able to carry magic ones, though.
"I can also sell you food or empty flasks for carrying liquids or powders."
"You can get really good protection from my chain mail armor.  It's very heavy though, and I would have to charge you 500 silvers for it."
"Ah!  The use of the dagger is a most skillful art.  Actually, this particular weapon is longer than most, but still easily concealable.  A bargain at 20 silvers."
"Adventuring rations aren't the tastiest food in the world, but they will keep you healthy and alert as you go along.  A pack of 5 rations will cost you just 5 silvers."
"Oh, no, I don't carry that sort of thing here.  If you want to get drunk, try the tavern."
"It's a very good idea to carry an empty flask or two, in case you want to pick up a liquid or something else that needs a container.  Our flasks are a great bargain at 2 silvers each."
"Oh, this book?  It's about an adventurer who is trying to become a Hero.  The title is 'Quest for Glory:  A Hero's Death'."
"Oh, you don't want those.  They're for the people who live here in town.  Adventurers just need adventuring equipment."
"The Sheriff and Otto protect this town.  The Sheriff used to be a real adventurer, you know!"
"Why, that's me!  Kaspar!"
"Hilde sells fine produce for a reasonable price."
"I don't like to talk about other people behind their backs."
I have pigs feet, brooms, rakes, handles, pots, teapots, eggs, linen, flour, etc.."
"I'm sorry, I don't have any of those for sale."
"I carry weapons, armor, daggers, rations, ale flasks, and various food stuffs."
"Aw, you wouldn't want to buy that."
Oh, I'm sorry! I didn't notice you coming in.
"Well, my adventurer friend!  Would you like to make another purchase?"
"Well, my adventurer friend!  Would you like to make a purchase this time?"
"My, you look like an adventurer!  We don't see too many around here.  The brigands tend to get rid of most adventurers before they make it to town."
"You may be in luck.  I carry a modest amount of adventurer's equipment behind the counter.  Would you like to make a purchase?"
"Oh, good! I do try to stock some things adventurers can use.  I actually would rather be an adventurer than a shopkeeper, you see."
"My items are pretty ordinary, though.  You probably already have most of them if you've done any adventuring at all."
"Now what was that you wanted again?"
Well, look around anyway.  You may find something you could use."

characters.name: Gate Gargoyle
characters.voiceDesc: An American male voice with a robotic edge, well-suited for science fiction and fantasy projects that require a synthetic, futuristic feel.

lines.text: "DO NOT DISTURB THE WIZARD AT REST!"
"AND YOU WANT TO BE A HERO?!"

"YOU DON'T GIVE UP EASILY,
DO YOU?"
"OH!  YOU AGAIN!"
"STAND FAST!"

"CAN YOU HEAR ME?
YOU HAVE THREE QUESTIONS."
"HERE COME YOUR QUESTIONS:"

"HE WHO WOULD THE WIZARD SEE
FIRST MUST ANSWER QUESTIONS THREE."
"BEAT IT, BUB!"
"WRONG!"

ASKING QUESTIONS
IS *MY* JOB, BUB!
"NO THIEVES ALLOWED!"
"THAT'S NOT NICE!"
"WHAT IS YOUR NAME?"
"WHAT IS YOUR FAVORITE COLOR?"
"WHAT IS YOUR QUEST?"
"WHO DO YOU SEEK HERE?"

"WHAT IS THE MEAN AIRSPEED
OF AN UNLADEN SWALLOW?"

"WHAT IS THE MEANING OF LIFE,
THE UNIVERSE, AND EVERYTHING?"

"IS IT FURTHER TO SHAPEIR,
OR BY CAMEL?"
"IS THIS A YES OR NO QUESTION?"
"WHAT IS THE BARON'S FIRST NAME?"
"WHOSE SPELL PROTECTS THE TOWN?"
"WHAT IS THE THIEVES' PASSWORD?"
"GOOD.  YOU'RE NOT A THIEF!"
THAT IS CORRECT.
THE WIZARD WILL NOW SEE YOU.

GO DIRECTLY UP TO THE TOWER.
DO NOT DALLY!

characters.name: Abdulla Doo
characters.voiceDesc: Middle-aged British male voice with a laid-back, velvety quality. Perfect for a relaxed, late-night radio talk show style.
