lines.text: "I am sorry, but I'm much too broken to talk about such things right now.  I have lost everything!"

"There were about twelve brigands, including a Minotaur, if you can believe it!  Their leader was wearing a hooded cloak.  I could not see the face, but he had a high-pitched voice.  There was also some sort of warlock who giggled a lot."
"Although worn with age, the brigand leader's cloak must have, at one time, been an exquisite garment.  No doubt it was stolen, as were all of my goods!"
"Perhaps it was my eyes that deceived me, but I could swear there was Gnome blood in that odd magician."
"The magician who travels with the brigands is no wizard."
"It is my hat."
"It is my turban."
"I am Abdulla Doo, son of Ali, grandson of Hasan, and formerly Master Merchant of Shapeir.  Now I am but a penniless burden upon my friends."
"Even though they, too, lost a fortune when the brigands stole from me, Shameen and Shema are caring for this frail shadow of a great man."
"Oh, I cannot bear to talk about my family at this sad time!"
"You must not have heard of the brigands who prey upon the innocent and unwary."
"All that I once owned is gone, alas!"
"Who would have thought that there could be such trouble entering this valley during the off-season?"
"My good friends Shema and Shameen, owners of this inn, are the finest of all the Katta people that ever graced Shapeir!  But for their kindness, I would have starved long since."
"If you have never seen such a beast before, you may count yourself lucky.  It is like a giant of a man with a bull's head."
"Two weeks ago, I was about to become wealthy beyond my dreams.  I, Abdulla Doo, would be the first merchant into the valley this year.  But my life was shattered by those vile brigands!"
"The band of brigands ambushed us just as we crossed the pass into the valley. They first used some magic which blinded us.  Then they overwhelmed my six guards and my assistants."
"All of my trade goods were taken from me, right before my very eyes!  Why the brigand leader spared my life, I do not know.  I am now but a beggar, living off the generosity of my friends."
"Ah, Shapeir!  Beautiful land of golden sands and shining sun! The heart of civilization!  Alas, she is plagued with fierce Djinni and Efreets, who seek to drive all men and Kattas from the land."
"But I can speak no more of the homeland I shall never see again.  Instead, I will die in this cold, forsaken land, bereft of all I love!"
"Alas, had I known magic, perhaps I could have turned the tide against those abominable brigands.  Their magic did not seem so strong, but they outnumbered my poor caravan by two to one!"
"My guards are all gone, killed or run away like dogs!  I, too, would be lying dead had not the strange brigand leader spared my life, for what reason I know not!"
"Your kindness overwhelms me.  I can tell you will someday be a great hero."
"Now you will have a secret.  Among the items they stole from me was a magic rug.  I alone know the words to command it."
"If you help recover my treasure, I will take you, Shameen, and Shema back to our land by way of the flying carpet.  Shapeir needs powerful heroes, too!"
"Oh, it is indeed sad and dangerous times we live in when a man who struggles daily to keep from starving should be robbed of all his earthly possessions!"
"Hello again, my friend."

characters.name: Stableman
characters.voiceDesc: Rich, engaging 30-year-old Irish male voice with a distinct accent, perfect for narrations, audiobooks, and instructional content.

lines.text: "Haven't you got anything better to do than hang around here?  Come back some other time you want to get rich."
"Don't tire me out with a bunch of questions.  Can't you see what a hard life I have?"
"Hey you!  It's bad enough you hang around here all day, but I sure ain't gonna let you sleep here before you've worked enough to earn it!"
Wait a minute!
"If you put in a day's work, and you wanna take a nap, it's OK with me."
"These horses, they act just like animals or somethin'."
"Just get out and wander around a little.  You'll find out."
"Don't ask me about anything to do with magic.  I don't wanna know."
"Those things scare me silly!"
"Ha-ha-ha-ha-ha!  Hee-hee-hee-ho-ho!"
"Sure!  C'mon in and grab a rake."
"Don't tire me out with a bunch of questions.  I don't need your comments, either.  Can't you see what a hard life I have?"
"Sorry, the Baron's horses aren't for sale."
"OK.  C'mon over here."
Now you're five silvers richer.
"WHAT DO YOU THINK THIS IS, A REST HOME???  GET TO WORK!  NOW!!!"
"Well, if you don't want to answer..."
"Good!  Come in here and take a rake."
Too bad!  I could use some help."
"That's what I'm asking YOU about, nimrod."
"I see you're back.  Need some work?"
"You lookin' for some work?"
"Quit fidgeting and answer me...yes or no!"

characters.name: Bartender (Aces & Eights)
characters.voiceDesc: A deep, gravelly Texan voice with the soul of the South and the charm of a fireside storyteller. He’s bold, warm, and proud—laid-back around the pit, rowdy with friends, and heartfelt when it matters. Ideal for Americana narratives, southern characters, or lively dialogue with grit and humor.

lines.text: "You'd better talk to Crusher."
Sit down first!
"OK. What'll you have?"
One well-aged house brew, coming up!
One mug of my fine Troll's Sweat, coming up!
If you want a mug of Dragon's Breath, house rules say it'll have to be cash up front!
"Thanks, buddy!"
"Hey, Crusher! Our friend here wants Dragon's Breath!"
Too bad, buddy. You're missing a real treat, for sure!
"Crusher, escort this squealer off the premises!"
"Yes...what?"
"No...what?"
"Hey! This is an honest establishment!  You want me to ask Crusher?"
"We've got some of the finest ale in the valley, only 1 silver."

lines.text:
"Brauggi I be, to boast of my boldness,
Strong as the storm that sends forth the snow.
Fiercer in fighting than foes in their fury,
Fear now this Frost Giant fighter and flee!"

"Find me some fruit for to mellow my mead horn,
Gift I will give of a gem that now glows --
Jewel from Jotunheim, flare of the frost flame,
Fetch to me fruit that will fill up my fists!"

"Near now is North, for the winter has wandered,
Bringing this brave one to barter so bold.
I journey from Jotunheim, home of the giants,
Source of all snowstorms, the sender of sleet."

"Highpoint of harvest, the finest of flavor,
Apples or oranges, peaches or pears."

"Mead is the mother's milk, mighty yet mellow,
That brings joy to Giants and madness to men."

"Glow of the frost flame that fills up the night field,
A jewel that is flawless, the finest of gems."

"Bored I become when the banter so blindly
Speaks of the subjects I care not about."
"Annoy me not over matters so minor."

"Waste not my wits, for I weary of words;
My axe it is asking for action and more."

"Far from the frost field, fares forth this fighter,
Hunger has hurled me hither from home.
My name it is known in the Northlands as Brauggi,
Barter with blade's clash, or bargain with me."

Fruit you have found, to fill all my food stores,
Thus filled the bargain, my gem you have bought.
Brauggi has bartered, and all has been answered;
The mead it may mellow, and now I head home.

"Fiercer than frost's bite, my fury is fearless,
My axe it is edged as is the ice.
This warrior is willing to wield now the war drum,
The battleaxe blazes and bellows for blood."

"Courage you carry, but skill it is scanty;
Face not this foe before fiercer you fight."
"In matters of magic, I am your master; Seek not to slay me with sendings so small."
